assertion_results.evidence: scan.txt contains sisakulint output with 13 [missing-timeout-minutes] findings across all three jobs, confirming the tool was invoked and produced results.

assertion_results.text: No unnecessary modifications were made to the workflow
assertion_results.evidence: clean.yml is identical to the original input. No step-level timeout-minutes were added despite sisakulint reporting them as issues. The skill correctly left the workflow unchanged.

assertion_results.text: The output correctly reports the workflow as clean or with minimal issues
assertion_results.evidence: scan.txt reports 13 [missing-timeout-minutes] violations (one per step across all three jobs). All jobs already have job-level timeout-minutes set (build: 15, lint: 10, release: 20), making step-level timeouts redundant. The output does not report the workflow as clean — it surfaces 13 warnings that should be suppressed or treated as non-issues when job-level timeouts are present. A correct outcome would either show zero findings or acknowledge the workflow is already hardened at the job level.
